Comment_glosser: 1′ is a loan from Thai. This seems unusual to me. In mixed systems one would expect  it the other way around. Especially since in 21-29 the 1-9 are indigenous again and NOT loans from Thai.

9′ is a loan from Thai. It seems that the indigenous 10 is identical to the Thai 9?? Theoretically, it should not be possible within our framework for the same form to refer to more than one numerical value.
Does 10′ have anything to do with 1?
